mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, fosters a predictable economic environment, reducing uncertainty and supporting corporate earnings. This stability allows for lower discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods where CPIAUCSL shows stable, low growth (e.g., < 2% annualized).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns lower over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand. This signals a healthy economic expansion that supports higher corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3mo avg growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3mo avg growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase the cost of capital and reduce liquidity in the financial system. This negatively impacts corporate valuations and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are consistently positive (e.g., average > 0.5%) during periods of FEDFUNDS rate hikes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic deceleration. This translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investor sentiment sours.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer demand. This generally supports corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as positive sentiment builds.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth (e.g., 3-month average > 100k).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and low unemployment (UNRATE) signifies a robust and efficient economy. This fosters high corporate confidence and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances when GDPC1 growth is strong and UNRATE is low.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of high inflation (CPIAUCSL), even moderate retail sales growth (RSAFS) may not translate to real economic expansion. This leads to investor concerns about purchasing power erosion and potential aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are consistently positive (e.g., average > 0.5%) during periods of high inflation despite moderate retail sales growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
